channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Italy rejects proposal to replace Iran in the World Cup
item.description: A recent suggestion from Paolo Zampolli, an Italian-American envoy for global relations, proposing that Italy replace Iran in this year’s World Cup has been met with widespread rejection in Italy. Zam...

item.title: ASIC cracks down on unlicensed financial influencers targeting Australians online
item.description: Australia’s corporate regulator, ASIC, is stepping up its efforts against financial influencers, or 'finfluencers', who provide unlicensed or misleading investment advice on social media platforms suc...

item.title: Quebec auction house sells rare 100-year-old hockey postcard collection for record amount
item.description: A Montreal-based auction house in Quebec has recently achieved a record-breaking sale by auctioning a collection of century-old hockey postcards. This unique set, valued for both its historical signif...

item.title: Luzon Faces Prolonged Drought Affecting 15 Provinces
item.description: As the dry season continues, 15 provinces in Luzon are now grappling with severe drought, according to the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA). The af...

item.title: Leadership crisis splits Katsina ADC as factions hold separate congresses
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) in Katsina State don enter serious leadership wahala after two different factions organise separate congresses and produce different party executives. The crisis ...

item.title: Rehabilitation of Kipchoge Keino Stadium Boosts Jobs and Community Unity in Eldoret
item.description: Kipchoge Keino Stadium in Eldoret, once neglected and underused, is undergoing major rehabilitation, transforming it into a modern sports facility with expanded seating, VIP stands, a canopy roof, and...

item.title: Federal Appeals Court Upholds Manslaughter Conviction of Conception Dive Boat Captain
item.description: A federal appeals court in California has denied a rehearing for Jerry Boylan, the 72-year-old captain of the Conception dive boat, who was convicted of manslaughter in connection with a 2019 fire tha...

item.title: How air pollution affects children’s lung development
item.description: The article explains how air pollution does more than trigger temporary breathing trouble in children; it can actually alter the way their lungs grow and function over time. Children are especially vu...

item.title: Tanzania launches KETAN project to expand newborn screening and improve sickle cell care
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has introduced the KETAN project as a major step to address sickle cell disease, which continues to affect thousands of children every year in the country. This initiative f...

item.title: Young girl rescues endangered Mexican axolotl in Wales
item.description: A 10-year-old girl named Evie discovered a rare Mexican axolotl, an endangered amphibian, under a bridge while spending the day near the River Ogmore in Bridgend, Wales. This is the first recorded sig...

item.title: Kwara PDP Youths Stage Protest Over Alleged Political Persecution of Bukola Saraki
item.description: Youths from the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) in Kwara State took to the streets of Ilorin on Thursday to protest what they described as the political victimisation of their leader, Bukola Saraki, ov...

item.title: Court Releases Protesters Arrested During Nairobi Fuel Price Demonstrations
item.description: A Nairobi court has released activist Julius Kamau together with nine other individuals who had been arrested during a disrupted fuel price protest in the city centre. Each of them was granted bail of...

item.title: Northern Kenya PWD Leaders Demand Inclusion in Government Appointments and Support Services
item.description: Leaders of people with disabilities (PWDs) in Northern Kenya have raised concerns over being sidelined in government appointments and political nominations. Hafid Maalim Ibrahim, chairman of PWDs in t...

item.title: Majority of MOI patients treated due to road traffic accidents, study shows
item.description: According to a recent study from the Muhimbili Orthopaedic and Neurosurgical Institute (MOI) in Tanzania, road traffic accidents continue to dominate hospital admissions, making up about 60 to 62 perc...

item.title: Atiku Urges Caution on Sokoto-Badagry Highway Loan Request
item.description: Former Vice-President Atiku Abubakar don express serious concern over President Bola Ahmed Tinubu request wey dey for Senate approval to get another $516 million foreign loan to fund parts of the Soko...

item.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.5, enhancing AI capabilities and moving toward multi-purpose application
item.description: OpenAI has launched GPT-5.5, its latest AI model, which the company describes as its most intuitive and capable model to date. According to OpenAI co-founder and president Greg Brockman, GPT-5.5 advan...

item.title: TCDC convenes stakeholders meeting to address governance and integrity issues in Tanzania's cooperative societies
item.description: The Tanzania Cooperative Development Commission (TCDC) is taking serious steps to rebuild public confidence in the country's cooperative societies following ongoing worries about ethics, accountabilit...

item.title: Disability Advocates Express Concerns Over NDIS Reforms by Health Minister Mark Butler
item.description: Disability advocates have voiced strong concerns following Health Minister Mark Butler’s announcement of significant reforms to the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS). The Albanese government...

item.title: The 10 Most Populous Tribes in Kenya and Their Estimated Population in 2026
item.description: Kenya is home to a rich diversity of tribes, with the Kikuyu, Luhya, Kalenjin, Luo, and Kamba communities being the largest. According to the 2019 population census, Kenya's total population stands at...

item.title: ICC Confirms Jurisdiction and Trial of Duterte Amid Wider Philippine Political Tensions
item.description: The article reports that the International Criminal Court (ICC) has firmly decided it has jurisdiction over former Philippine president Rodrigo Duterte’s case related to alleged crimes against humanit...
